verses.sanskrit: सीताम् अपश्यन् धर्मात्मा शोक उपहत चेतनः ।विललाप महाबाहू रामः कमल लोचनः ॥३-६२-१॥
verses.text:
In not seeing Seetha that virtue-souled Rama's sagacity is marred by his anguish and
he bewailed with his lotus-like eyes that are reddening like a pair of lotuses, raising is long
arms. [3-62-1]

verses.sanskrit: पश्यन् इव च ताम् सीताम् अपश्यन् मदन अर्दितः ।उवाच राघवो वाक्यम् विलाप आश्रय दुर्वचम् ॥३-६२-२॥
verses.text:
Even if Raghava is not able to see Seetha in his presence he started talking to her
in a kind of inarticulate wailing, as wailing pampered his articulacy because his speech took
shelter of wailing instinct, and as he is cowed down by Love-god, and thus he started to vent out
his heart in this way. [3-62-2]

verses.sanskrit: त्वम् अशोकस्य शाखाभिः पुष्प प्रिय तराअ प्रिये ।अवृणोषि शरीरम् ते मम शोक विवर्धिनी ॥३-६२-३॥
verses.text:
"Flowers fascinate you very much, my dear, hence you veil yourself with the full
bloomed branches of Ashoka tree, but that alone is amplifying my anguish because you both have
presently became tormentors. [3-62-3]

verses.sanskrit: कदली काण्ड सदृशौ कदल्या संवृता उभौ ।ऊरू पश्यामि ते देवि न असि शक्ता निगूहितुम् ॥३-६२-४॥
verses.text:
"Maybe, both your thighs liken to the stalks of banana plants, thinking so, now you
have lapped them in grovy banana plants, but I can distinguish which is which, thus now I caught
sight of them, oh, lady, you are inapt at least to cover them from me. [3-62-4]

verses.sanskrit: कर्णिकार वनम् भद्रे हसंती देवि सेवसे ।अलम् ते परिहासेन मम बाधावहेन वै ॥३-६२-५॥
verses.text:
"Oh, lady, you are facetiously glorying in the boscage of fully bloomed Karnikaara
trees which is really wafting worry to me, rather than the fragrance of those flowers oh, glorious
lady, enough, enough is this facetiousness of yours. [3-62-5]

verses.sanskrit: विशेषेण आश्रमस्थाने हासो अयम् न प्रशस्यते ।अवगच्छामि ते शीलम् परिहास प्रियम् प्रिये ॥३-६२-६॥
verses.text:
"I am aware of your humour, lady, and I know that you are jocose, but in a place
like hermitage unmerited is this sort of raillery, even if it is good-natured. Hence, you come
back, oh, wide-eyed one, your cottage is empty. [3-62-6, 7a]

verses.sanskrit: आगच्छ त्वम् विशालाक्षी शून्यो अयम् उटजः तव ।सु व्यक्तम् राक्षैः सीता भक्षिता वा हृता अपि वा ॥३-६२-७॥
verses.text:
"Oh, Lakshmana, very evidently demons have either gorged up Seetha, or perhaps
abducted her, because she is not returning to me who am whiny indeed for her. [3-62-7b, 8a]

verses.sanskrit: न हि सा विलपंतम् माम् उपसम्प्रैति लक्ष्मण ।एतानि मृग यूधानि स अश्रु नेत्राणि लक्ष्मण ॥३-६२-८॥
verses.text:
"Indeed these teary-eyed mobs of deer look as if to explain that nightwalkers have
gluttonised my lady. [3-62-8b, 9a]

verses.sanskrit: शंशन्ति इव हि मे देवीम् भक्षिताम् रजनीचरैः ।हा मम आर्ये क्व याता असि हा साध्वि वर वर्णिनि ॥३-६२-९॥
verses.text:
"Ha! My graceful lady, to where you have strayed now... Ha! Chastely and best
complexioned lady, now the ambition of my queen mother Kaikeyi will be fulfilled, as I breath my
last owing to your straying... [3-62-8b, 9a]

verses.sanskrit: हा स कामा अद्य कैकेयी देवि मे अद्य भविष्यति ।सीताया सह निर्यातो विना सीताम् उपागतः ॥३-६२-१०॥
verses.text:
"I have come to forests with Seetha and have to go back to Ayodhya without Seetha.
How, in all but name, can I step into an oblivion called my palace-chambers? [3-62-9b, 10a]

verses.sanskrit: कथम् नाम प्रवेक्ष्यामि शून्यम् अन्तः पुरम् मम ।निर्वीर्य इति लोको माम् निर्दयः च इति वक्ष्यति ॥३-६२-११॥
verses.text:
"People will denounce me as a vigourless and pitiless person, and my ineptitude will
indeed be self-evident, for Seetha is led away from me by some tactical being... [3-62-10b,
11a]

verses.sanskrit: कातरत्वम् प्रकाशम् हि सीता अपनयनेन मे ।निवृत्त वन वासः च जनकम् मिथिल अधिपम् ॥३-६२-१२॥
verses.text:
"When the king of Mithila Janaka asks after the wellbeing of all the three of us
after the completion of forest living, how do I have the face to stand him? [3-62-11b, 12a]

verses.sanskrit: कुशलम् परिपृच्छन्तम् कथम् शक्षे निरीक्षितुम् ।विदेह रजो नूनम् माम् दृष्ट्वा विरहितम् तया ॥३-६२-१३॥
verses.text:
"On seeing me without Seetha the king of Videha will be distraught by the perishing
of his daughter, and he defiantly goes under the preponderance of perplexity... [3-62-12b, 13a]

verses.sanskrit: सुता विनाश संतप्तो मोहस्य वशम् एष्यति ।अथवा न गमिष्यामि पुरीम् भरत पालितम् ॥३-६२-१४॥
verses.text:
"Instead, I prefer not to go to the city Ayodhya that is ruled by Bharata, because
it must be comforting to one and all under his rulership, but not to me as Seetha will not be with
me... else if, that end of my life occurs now and if I were to go to heaven, even that heaven will
be a void to me without her... I believe so... [3-62-13b, 14a]

verses.sanskrit: स्वर्गो अपि हि तया हीनः शून्य एव मतो मम ।तत् माम् उत्सृज्य हि वने गच्छ अयोध्या पुरीम् शुभाम् ॥३-६२-१५॥
verses.text:
"Hence, Lakshmana, you go back to that auspicious city Ayodhya forsaking me in
forests, because I have no existence without Seetha, isn't it! [3-62-14b, 15a]

verses.sanskrit: न तु अहम् ताम् विना सीताम् जीवेयम् हि कथंचन ।गाढम् आश्लिष्य भरतो वाच्यो मत् वचनात् त्वया ॥३-६२-१६॥
verses.text:
"On tightly hugging Bharata you shall tell him these words as I have said, 'Rama
authorises you to reign the earth...' [3-62-16]

verses.sanskrit: अनुज्ञातो असि रामेण पालय इति वसुंधराम् ।अंबा च मम कैकेयी सुमित्रा च त्वया विभो ॥३-६२-१७॥
verses.text:
"Oh, efficacious Lakshmana, on revering my mothers Kaika, Sumitra, and Kausalya
justifiably you tell them my good bye, and you as the one who effectuates whatever is assigned to
you, you have to effortfully protect my mother Kausalya by doing whatever she says. [3-62-17b,
18]

verses.sanskrit: कौसल्या च यथा न्यायम् अभिवाद्या मम अज्ञया ।रक्षणीया प्रयत्नेन भवता सा उक्त कारिणा ॥३-६२-१८॥
सीतायाः च विनाशो अयम् मम च अमित्र सूदन ।विस्तरेण जनन्या विनिवेद्य त्वया भवेत् ॥३-६२-१९॥
verses.text:
"Oh, the subjugator of unfriendly, Lakshmana... you shall clearly inform in detail
about this perish of Seetha, also that of mine, to our mothers. [3-62-19]

verses.sanskrit: इति विलपति राघवो तु दीनोवनम् उपगम्य तया विना सु केश्या ।भय विकल मुखः तु लक्ष्मणो अपिव्यथित मना भृशम् आतुरो बभूव ॥३-६२-२०॥
verses.text:
Thus Raghava, the dejected, neared every corner of the forest in his search, and
bewailed because that lady with best plaits, Seetha, is not found and missing from him. Even
Lakshmana became whey-faced, frantic-hearted, highly overwrought, by the fear of uncertainty
looming large on them. [3-62-20]
